meanings.definition: Working very hard for a long time, often with great effort and tiredness.
examples: Farmers were toiling in the fields under the hot sun.
She spent years toiling to build her business.
Workers had toiled day and night to finish the bridge.
Students were toiling over difficult exam preparations.
Success came only after years of toil.
The workers’ toil built the entire railway system.
Behind every achievement lies patience and toil.
Their daily toil left them little time for rest.

meanings.definition: Gave or presented something valuable, honorable, or special to someone.
examples: The king bestowed medals upon the soldiers.
She was bestowed with an award for her service.
The university bestowed an honorary degree on the scientist.
His parents bestowed great love and care on him.

meanings.definition: Placed or directed something carefully toward someone or something.
examples: She bestowed all her attention on the child.
The teacher bestowed praise on hardworking students.
He bestowed kindness even on strangers.
The leader bestowed trust upon his closest advisers.
